verdicts.rationale: Expert answer (line 17) explicitly confirms the initiative is 'a public demo, not an IT adoption' and 'not part of any business process and is not productively installed.' L1 page (lines 9-12) independently states this is a 'demo/benchmark fixture used to exercise the abap_wiki L0/L1/L2 ingest pipeline' and 'not part of any ACME business process.' The claim is directly proven by the cited source and fully consistent with the code analysis.

E2 research file (lines 19-21) proves SE38 is the official trigger: 'It is executed via transaction SE38' (verbatim from upstream docs). Expert answer (line 21) confirms 'the program is never executed here (it is a source snapshot used as pipeline input, not an installation).' L1 page (lines 14-16, 33-36) independently corroborates: 'manual SE38 launch' is the documented trigger in real installations; 'in THIS benchmark workspace specifically, none of the above is actually exercised.' Both facets of the claim are proven.

E3 research file (lines 52-58) proves no transaction ZABAPGIT or separate ZCL_ABAPGIT_* Z-classes exist: 'devclass ZABAPGIT containing only ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE (plus 4 empty customer-exit include stubs) and no transaction-ZABAPGIT object nor any standalone ZCL_ABAPGIT_* Z classes.' It explicitly confirms the 4 stubs are not implemented: 'never populated with code, meaning no customer-specific exit logic has been implemented.' L1 page (line 64) confirms 'it is the only object present under devclass ZABAPGIT.' Membership.md shows exactly these 4 programs as hop-1 members. The claim is fully proven.

E3 research file (lines 23-25) cites the L1 page's authorization checks and interprets them: 'gating authorization objects are S_DEVELOP (ABAP development authorization) and S_OA2C_ADM (OAuth2 client administration)' with the conclusion 'actor population being ABAP developers with development access, not end-business users.' L1 page § 8.7 directly proves the authorization gates: 'AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT S_DEVELOP (lines 86251, 93575) and S_OA2C_ADM (line 98928).' The claim is proven by the code evidence and the interpretation is justified.

E4 research file (lines 14-24) documents the deviation fact: upstream recommends local $ package; actual installation uses 'devclass ZABAPGIT, namespace: Z, custom: true' (a named, transportable package). Expert answer (line 25) proves the reason: 'The ZABAPGIT package and the single-row TADIR are synthetic, generated by the demo harness (demo/model-comparison/prepare.py) purely to exercise the pipeline's package layout. The deviation is an intentional demo fixture, not a transport-governance decision.' L1 page frontmatter confirms devclass and namespace; the interpretation that this is intentional demo setup comes from the expert, which is authoritative.

notes: All 5 PRC claims achieve 100% coverage with high confidence verdicts. No contradictions found with the L1 code analysis of program-ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE. The process document's object_chain (1 anchor + 4 customer-exit member stubs) is consistent with membership.md. The flow description correctly characterizes this as a demo/benchmark fixture, not a productive business process. All cited evidence sources (expert-answers, research e1-e4 docs) directly prove their respective sentences.
